title: Nice 14-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Nice 14-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Nice, France: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Musée Matisse and Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild. Prices from $2355 per person (estimate).
highlights: Day trip to Monte Carlo with casino visit
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Nice, France — Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 27-28°C) and mild winters (highs around 13°C), with over 300 sunny days a year. May-June and September for warm weather without peak-August crowds and prices. This itinerary blends Musée Matisse, Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild, Promenade des Anglais with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Musée Matisse, Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild, Promenade des Anglais
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Nice
itinerary.description: Land at Nice Côte d'Azur Airport (NCE) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Musée Matisse — Collection spanning Henri Matisse's career, in a 17th-century villa. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Nice: Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild & Monaco and Monte Carlo day trip
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild — Belle Époque villa and gardens overlooking the bay, Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat. In the afternoon, continue to Monaco and Monte Carlo day trip — Half-day excursion to the neighboring principality. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Monaco and Monte Carlo day trip

itinerary.title: Nice: Èze village and perfumery tour & Musée National Marc Chagall
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Èze village and perfumery tour — Clifftop medieval village with a guided fragrance-house visit. In the afternoon, continue to Musée National Marc Chagall — Dedicated collection of Marc Chagall's biblical-themed works. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Monaco and Monte Carlo
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Monaco and Monte Carlo, a popular side trip from Nice. Return to Nice in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Monaco and Monte Carlo

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Russian Orthodox Cathedral of Nice — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Nice: Promenade des Anglais & Vieux Nice (Old Town)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Promenade des Anglais — Iconic palm-lined seafront walkway along the Baie des Anges. In the afternoon, continue to Vieux Nice (Old Town) — Warren of pastel-colored lanes, markets, and Baroque churches. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Èze village
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Èze village, a popular side trip from Nice. Return to Nice in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Nice: Colline du Château & Cours Saleya Market
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Colline du Château — Hilltop park and viewpoint above the Old Town, ruins of a former castle. In the afternoon, continue to Cours Saleya Market — Daily flower and produce market in the Old Town. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Port Lympia's pastel harbor houses — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Cannes and Antibes
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Cannes and Antibes, a popular side trip from Nice. Return to Nice in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Cannes and Antibes

itinerary.title: Nice: Musée Matisse & Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Musée Matisse — Collection spanning Henri Matisse's career, in a 17th-century villa. In the afternoon, continue to Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild — Belle Époque villa and gardens overlooking the bay, Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Nice: Monaco and Monte Carlo day trip & Èze village and perfumery tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Monaco and Monte Carlo day trip — Half-day excursion to the neighboring principality. In the afternoon, continue to Èze village and perfumery tour — Clifftop medieval village with a guided fragrance-house visit. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Monaco and Monte Carlo day trip

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Cimiez neighborhood Roman ruins — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Musée National Marc Chagall or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to NCE for your departure flight.

travelTips: Take the coastal train, not a car, for day trips to Monaco, Èze, or Cannes — parking is scarce and pricey.
Try socca, Nice's signature chickpea-flour street food, from a stall in the Old Town.
Book Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild and Èze perfumery tours ahead in peak summer.
Bring water shoes or sandals — Nice's beaches are pebble, not sand.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Swimwear and a beach towel — Nice's beaches are pebble, so water shoes help
Smart-casual clothing for dinner and casino visits in Monaco
Sun protection for the long, sunny Riviera days
thingsToDo: Monaco and Monte Carlo day trip
Hiking the Cap Ferrat coastal path
Paragliding over the bay from the surrounding hills
Nice beach days (pebble beaches with paid loungers)
Parc Phoenix botanical and zoological park
thingsToAvoid: Renting a car for day trips along the coast — traffic and parking are much worse than the train.
Expecting sandy beaches — pack accordingly for pebbles.
Skipping a reservation at well-known restaurants like La Petite Maison, especially in summer.
topRestaurants: Restaurant Flaveur — Modern French-Italian fine dining ($$$$)
topCafes: La Part des Anges wine bar
instagramSpots: Colline du Château viewpoint over the bay
familyActivities: Nice beach days (pebble beaches with paid loungers)
Parc Phoenix botanical and zoological park
adventureActivities: Hiking the Cap Ferrat coastal path
Paragliding over the bay from the surrounding hills
luxuryExperiences: Day trip to Monte Carlo with casino visit
Private yacht day charter along the Riviera coast
Suite stay at a Belle Époque promenade hotel
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset stroll along the Promenade des Anglais
Private day trip to Èze and the perfumeries
Dinner in a Vieux Nice courtyard restaurant
budgetExperiences: Free walk along the Promenade des Anglais
Vieux Nice and Cours Saleya market wander
Free viewpoint hike up Colline du Château
localEtiquette: A polite "Bonjour" on entering any shop or café is expected before making a request.
Meals, especially dinner, are a leisurely affair — service can feel slow by design.
Beachwear is for the beach and promenade only, not restaurants in town.
localTransportGuide: Nice's tram and bus network covers the city well and connects to the airport; the Promenade des Anglais and Old Town are best explored on foot. Trains along the coast make day trips to Monaco, Èze, Cannes, and Antibes quick and scenic.
bestTimeToVisit: May-June and September for warm weather without peak-August crowds and prices.
weather: Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 27-28°C) and mild winters (highs around 13°C), with over 300 sunny days a year.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period for US, UK, Canadian, Australian, and EU passport holders. Other nationalities, including India, should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~10.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Nice?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period for US, UK, Canadian, Australian, and EU passport holders. Other nationalities, including India, should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Nice?
faqs.answer: May-June and September for warm weather without peak-August crowds and prices. Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 27-28°C) and mild winters (highs around 13°C), with over 300 sunny days a year.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Nice safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Nice is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Nice?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Marco Brown
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Cours Saleya Market and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Emma Müller
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Our trip to Nice was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Colline du Château was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Sarah Kowalski
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Nice is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Èze village and perfumery tour. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Mohammed Sharma
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Promenade des Anglais alone was worth the trip, but combined with Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Ethan Ibrahim
